data.title: Charles Schwab makes minority investment in estate planning platform Wealth.com
data.description:
With the goal of enhancing its wealth management offering, Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) made a minority investment in AI-powered estate planning platform Wealth.com, it said on Wednesday.

Wealth.com enables financial advisors to help clients optimize their estate plans or give clients without an estate plan the ability to immediately self-create robust legal documents (e.g., wills and revocable trusts) in all 50 U.S. states and D.C. 

Schwab's (NYSE:SCHW) investment is set to make this process easier.

"Wealth.com is an important first step in building out a support ecosystem for our advisor clients as they respond to investors’ needs, while also providing a scalable and easy-to-use solution for our retail clients to meet more of their financial needs at Charles Schwab,” Charles Schwab President and CEO Rick Wurster said in a statement.

As an extension of the investment, the two companies are also developing opportunities to offer access to Wealth.com’s estate planning tools to Schwab’s (SCHW) clients. Additional information and launch details will be shared at a later date.

Terms of the investment have not been disclosed.

SCHW shares slipped 0.3% in premarket trading.

data.title: SA Charts: Semiconductor sales dip in Feb sequentially, Americas falls most among regions
data.description:
Worldwide semiconductor sales were $54.9 billion during the month of February, noting a sequential decline for the second straight month. 

February sales decreased 2.9% from the previous month but rose 17.1% compared to the same period last year, the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics data said. 

The Americas, which account for nearly half of the semiconductor sales, saw the biggest month-on-month dip in sales at 4.6%, to $18.64B in February. 

 

 

As the Trump administration continues to tweak its tariff policies, the Department of Commerce is now looking into the potential impact of semiconductor imports on national security, likely setting the stage for tariffs on the sectors.

Semiconductor sales in China and Japan noted a 3.1% sequential decline in February. Sales in Asia Pacific held steady versus January. 

Compared to last year, the Americas recorded a 48.4% jump in semiconductor sales, while Asia Pacific was the runner-up at a 10.8% growth pace. China saw a 5.6% year-on-year rise in sales in February.

data.title: Stock index futures fall as Nvidia's China licensing requirement fuels trade war concerns
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Wednesday, as worries about a U.S.-China trade war rose on news that Nvidia will need a special license to ship graphic processing units to China.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.4% and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.82%.

The core retail sales beat consensus in March, while the headline number came in line

Wall Street on Tuesday ended slightly lower in a choppy trading session, as a boost from positive bank earnings petered out amid more caution over developments surrounding tariffs.

"After a strong start to the week, the market mood turned more negative again yesterday, as tensions between the US and China showed signs of further escalation," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"So after a brief period of greater stability in markets, that reminded investors about the ongoing risks of escalation, raising fears that the trade war could still get worse from here," Allen added.

Nvidia (NVDA) fell 5.4% in premarket trade after it received a notice from the U.S. government that it needs a special license to ship its H20 graphic processing units or related hardware to China, and such a license will be required indefinitely.

"Some politicians may believe trade is more important than it is. Trade does matter economically, but a 'made in China' product in a store does not mean all the price paid goes to China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

President Donald Trump has also directed an investigation to determine if U.S. critical minerals imports "threaten to impair national security," which could lead to tariffs or restrictions on the sector.

On the economic side, traders will keep an eye on the March industrial production data, which is expected to come in at -0.2% M/M.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also expected to speak later in the day.

data.title: bluebird bio confirms Ayrmid has not delivered binding offer, shares dive 9%
data.description:
• bluebird bio (NASDAQ:BLUE) Board on Wednesday reaffirmed unanimous recommendation in support of transaction with Carlyle and SK Capital and recommends all stockholders tender into the current agreement by May 2, 2025.
  • bluebird bio (NASDAQ:BLUE) said Ayrmid has not submitted a binding proposal to acquire bluebird and has not obtained necessary financing.
  • The company's shares fell over 9% in premarket trading. 
  • “Ayrmid’s proposal remains highly conditional, despite an extension to the previously agreed-upon timeline to complete confirmatory diligence and submit a binding offer,” said Mark Vachon, chairman of the bluebird bio Board of Directors.
  • “bluebird has engaged with Ayrmid on two separate occasions—neither of which has resulted in a binding or fully-financed offer."
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GameSquare enters partnership with GGTech Entertainment
data.description:
• GameSquare Holdings (NASDAQ:GAME) has announced that it has formed a strategic partnership with GGTech Entertainment.
  • The strategic partnership combines two industry powerhouses that feature global scale, blue-chip customer bases, best-in-class capabilities and industry leading technologies.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, GameSquare will initially license GAMERGY in the U.S. from GGTech.
  • GameSquare will develop and expand GGTech's gaming related events and operations in the U.S., which leverages GAMERGY's more than nine years of history across nine cities and on three continents.
  • "This partnership aligns with our long-term strategy of scaling our growing agency and experiences business. By partnering with top-tier brands, gaming communities, and major festivals across the U.S., we have positioned GameSquare as a premier provider of innovative and culturally relevant experiences that resonate with next-gen audiences." said Justin Kenna, CEO of GameSquare.

data.title: Hertz Global soars after Pershing Square takes a large stake; Avis Budget also higher
data.description:
Hertz Global (NASDAQ:HTZ) rallied in premarket trading after Pershing Square Holdings (OTCPK:PSHZF) disclosed in an SEC filing that it held a stake of 12.7 million shares in the car rental company to represent a stake of about 4.1% based on the 306.8 million shares outstanding. 

The fund is managed by Pershing Square Capital Management, L.P., led by Bill Ackman, a well-known activist investor. Pershing Square Holdings (OTCPK:PSHZF) also has large positions in Brookfield Corporation, Restaurant Brands International (QSR), Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), Howard Hughes Holdings (HHH), Alphabet (GOOG), and Nike (NKE).

Shares of Hertz Global (NASDAQ:HTZ) jumped 20.0% in premarket action to $4.38 vs. the 52-week range of $2.47 to $6.65. Short interest on HTZ stands at 17.3% of the total float, which could add to volatility. Avis Budget (CAR) was up 4.1% in the early session.

data.title: Goldman cools on Target as discretionary spending slows
data.description:
With a product mix more heavily skewed towards discretionary items, Goldman Sachs sees Target (NYSE:TGT) more vulnerable to a decline in consumer spending compared to peers like Walmart (WMT), BJ’s Wholesale Club (BJ), and Costco (COST). And with a recovery in growth for discretionary categories in the near term less likely, Goldman moves to the sidelines on the retailer, downgrading Target (NYSE:TGT) to Neutral from Buy.

“In our view, there is more downside risk to EPS than upside given potential top line deleverage and risk of tariffs,” Goldman said in Wednesday’s research note, pointing to recent data from HundredX and Placer which shows Target’s (NYSE:TGT) sales may already be slowing as the macro environment grows more volatile.

Just as consumers were beginning to see a light at the end of the inflation tunnel, tariffs are taking another swing at spending habits.

“We’re asking consumers to play the long game, but from an inflation perspective, consumers have been playing the long game for years…they’re worn out,” said Matt Priest, CEO of Footwear Distributors and Retailers of America in a recent interview.

This leaves retailers like Target (TGT) with the hard choice of raising prices for an already price-sensitive consumer, or take a hit to margins, hoping that tariffs are only temporary.

And although spending around seasonal events will continue to plump up quarterly results, “the decline in overall discretionary spending will continue to weigh on Target’s top-line for the near term,” Goldman warns.  

For the time being, Goldman's Neutral outlook on Target (TGT) stands out among Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors, all of which currently share a consensus Buy rating for Target, albeit with a score of just 3.63-3.90. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating is more in-line with Goldman, rating the stock as a Hold with a Quant score of 3.04 out of 5.

Shares are down ~2% before Wednesday’s opening bell.

data.title: Prologis Q1 earnings top consensus; dials back outlook on development starts, dispositions
data.description:
Prologis (NYSE:PLD) delivered stronger-than-expected Q1 earnings and revenue on Wednesday and reaffirmed its 2025 earnings guidance, but lowered expectations for dispositions and development starts as its customers become wary amid policy uncertainty.

The industrial REIT reaffirmed its guidance for 2025 core FFO per share at $5.65-$5.81, compared with the average analyst estimate of $5.75.

Spending on acquisitions was reaffirmed at $750M-$1.25B, but its outlook for dispositions was revised downward to $250M-$500M from its previous outlook of $1.00B-$1.50B. Development starts outlook was changed to $1.50B-$2.00B from $2.25B-$2.75B.

"In the near term, policy uncertainty is making customers more cautious," said co-founder and CEO Hamid R. Moghadam. "But over the long term, limited new supply and high construction costs support continued rent growth."

Q1 core FFO per share of $1.42, exceeding the consensus estimate of $1.38, decreased from $1.50 in Q4 2024 and increased from $1.28 in last year’s Q1.

Revenue of $2.14B, beating the $1.99B consensus estimate, fell from $2.20B in the previous quarter and rose from $1.96B a year ago.

Adjusted EBITDA of $1.77B slid from $2.11B in Q4 and climbed from $1.60B in Q1 2024.

Average occupancy dipped to 94.9% from 95.6% in Q4.

Cash same-store net operating income declined to 6.2% Y/Y vs. 6.7% growth in Q4.

Conference call at 12:00 PM ET.
